meta.title: Building Your First Atomic Component
meta.excerpt: Learn to create reusable input components following atomic design principles

props.text: A FormInput atom needs to accept a label, name, error message, and all standard input props while maintaining accessibility.

props.title: 💡 Hint
props.body: Think about the interface first. What props does a reusable input need?

props.text: Start by defining a TypeScript interface that extends HTML input attributes.

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: Extending React.InputHTMLAttributes gives us all standard input props (onChange, value, etc.) without manually typing them.

props.text: Now implement the component with proper accessibility attributes.

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: The aria-invalid and aria-describedby attributes help screen readers understand error states. Props spreading allows consumers to pass any HTML input attribute.
